signals.reasoning: Cover the live MON short immediately. The trade is now materially underwater, it has fully given back peak profits, and the board is clearly bullish rather than bearish.
BTC remains stable but does not offer enough edge to outrank the stronger post-cover opportunities.
ETH is constructive but still not offering a clean enough directional setup to justify fresh risk.
SOL has some relative strength, but the mechanical edge is weaker than the best long candidates on this sheet.
ZEREBRO still lacks the liquidity quality and structural edge needed for a high-confidence entry.
MAVIA is a cleaner bearish fade because funding is very positive, crowding is high, and the market quality is poor enough that upside continuation looks fragile.
DASH is one of the better long setups after flattening MON because mechanics are strongly bullish and funding is supportive for a squeeze-style continuation.
HMSTR has weak edge and very poor execution quality, so it stays on the sidelines.
COMP is the best long on the board because it combines the strongest mechanical score with deeply negative funding, making it the cleanest squeeze-style continuation candidate.
STBL is a valid fade candidate because funding is extremely positive and crowding is high, which is a dangerous combination in a thin market.
CHILLGUY is still extended and crowded, but the risk-reward is not clean enough to justify a fresh trade here.
